newsSince: Order book crossed 4.5 GW as of June 2026 (incl. a 1,500 MW MoU with Inox Clean) and FY26 EBITDA-margin guidance was raised to 18-19%, with new nacelle/hub, transformer and planned blade capacity. The June 22 EGM approved the divestment of Inox Green Energy Services shares. Constructive for power-buildout exposure.

sources.label: Inox Wind Q2 FY26 earnings call / order book & guidance (Alpha Spread)

verdicts.rankRationale: Scaling/electricity lens rewards power-capacity buildout for AI compute. Order book >4.5 GW (June) plus raised FY26 EBITDA-margin guidance to 18-19% and new capacity strengthen the wind-equipment power-supply thesis - genuine fundamental improvement vs the June 8 hold. Modest +2; India wind is indirect AI-power exposure.
verdicts.bull: Record >4.5 GW order book, raised margin guidance and capacity expansion deliver multi-year power-equipment growth feeding grid/AI electricity demand.
verdicts.neutral: Strong order momentum, but execution/working-capital and -26% YTD reflect lingering balance-sheet skepticism.
verdicts.bear: Wind equipment is an indirect AI-power proxy; Indian renewables history of delays and the Inox Green overhang.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Order-book execution and margin delivery toward FY26 guidance
verdicts.primaryRisk: Project-execution slippage; capital-intensity and group-related-party risk
